sub: Putting drone traffic on the map — literally

title: Geodata for Flight Route Planning
desc: A drone route is a query against many geographic layers at once

body.card: The layers a route planner needs
items: Geo-zones — no-fly and restricted zones (airports, hospitals, prisons, nature reserves)
Terrain & obstacles — elevation models, buildings, power lines, cranes
Population density — ground risk for the Specific category (SORA) depends on who is below
Weather layers — wind fields and precipitation as time-dependent rasters
Live traffic — other drones and manned aviation, streamed in real time (Modules 4, 5, 7)
Live positions rendered on the map — the FlightAware picture from Module 1, now for unmanned traffic
Track history, planned route and actual route in one view: instant anomaly detection for operators

title: Applications & the German Data Source
desc: Logistics, rescue services — and where the official geo-zones come from

items: Logistics — corridor planning for delivery routes, automatically re-routed around temporary geo-zones
Rescue services — fastest legal route for a medical-supply drone, computed against live airspace data
Inspection & monitoring — pipelines, railways and power lines flown along precise geo-referenced paths
Germany's official digital platform for unmanned aviation (BMDV / DFS)
Interactive map of all geographic zones relevant for drone flights — the authoritative source for German operations
Machine-readable access makes it a natural data layer for every route planner discussed on the previous slide
